description: Reference-machine performance baseline for the hook hot path (R6-02). Recorded by `node scripts/perf-baseline.js --slo --json` (plus a --surface=dual-cold capture); grading thresholds live in qa/perf/slo.json, the operator flow in spec/OPERATOR.md §O9. Values are per-event totals across all hooks of that event (dual = BOTH physical copies), in ms; p95 totals are sums of per-hook p95s (conservative). Compare future --slo runs against these when validating hook hot-path changes; re-record on the reference machine when hook count, timeouts, or the yield mechanism change.
sloVerdict: PASS (2 rounds x 15 runs/hook, both rounds stable; PreToolUse round-to-round p95 delta 1%)
notes: dual-warm PreToolUse overhead 182.9 ms = the N-01 yield cost for 5 losing-copy hooks + the winner's cache checks (~37 ms/hook, jq-only); the slo.json 400 ms cap is ~2.2x this baseline.
dual-cold is barely worse than dual-warm on the non-triggering echo event (387.5 vs 371.7 ms): post-R0-04 the cold path costs a second full hook pass, not a node/sha256/codex probe per hook.
Worst per-hook p95 is 2% of its registered timeout (memory-read-check, 72.6 ms / 3000 ms) — the 0.5 headroom fraction sits ~25x above today's slowest hook.
